Boynton Beach Home Loans

FHA loans in Boynton Beach, Florida

Boynton Beach offers a more affordable foothold on the Palm Beach County coast than neighbors like Boca Raton, with a revitalizing downtown, established neighborhoods, and a range of condos and 55-and-over communities. Because Palm Beach is a designated high-cost county, the FHA limit sits above Florida's floor — which, combined with Boynton's relative value, makes FHA genuinely workable here.

Palm Beach County FHA limit (1-unit)$667,000

2026 single-family

Minimum down3.5%

With a 580+ score

Min credit score580

Property types1–4 units

Owner-occupied

What the FHA limit means in Boynton Beach

Palm Beach County is a designated high-cost area, so its 2026 single-family FHA limit is $667,000, above the $541,287 statewide floor — meaning you can finance higher-priced Boynton Beach homes than in most of Florida. Confirm Palm Beach County's exact limit with HUD's official FHA loan-limit lookup. Combined with Boynton's relative affordability, that higher limit covers a good share of local homes and condos.

Relative value on the Palm Beach coast

Boynton Beach is where many Palm Beach County buyers find FHA-friendly prices. The area has lots of condos (including 55+ communities) — and FHA requires the building to be FHA-approved or the unit to qualify for Single-Unit Approval; see our condo guide. Note FHA is for primary residences you'll occupy. Single-family neighborhoods are simpler purchases, and Palm Beach's higher limit gives you room.

Down payment help for Boynton Beach buyers

You don't have to cover the down payment alone. FHA allows gift funds, sellers can contribute up to 6% toward closing costs, and Boynton Beach buyers can often pair FHA with assistance from the Florida Housing Finance Corporation and Palm Beach County's local SHIP-funded programs. Funding and eligibility change frequently, so we'll help you confirm what's currently available — see our FHA down payment assistance guide.

Neighborhoods we serve in Boynton Beach

We help buyers throughout Boynton Beach and Palm Beach County, including downtown Boynton, Leisureville, and the beachfront communities. Wherever you're looking, we'll explain your FHA loan limit, rate, and monthly payment — and get you pre-approved fast.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

What is the FHA loan limit in Boynton Beach (Palm Beach County) for 2026?+
Palm Beach County is a high-cost area, so its 2026 FHA limit is $667,000, above the $541,287 Florida floor. Confirm the exact figure with HUD's official lookup.
Is Boynton Beach more affordable than Boca Raton for FHA?+
Generally yes — Boynton tends to offer better value, so FHA's limit stretches further here than in pricier parts of Palm Beach County.
Can I buy a Boynton Beach condo with FHA?+
Yes, if the building is FHA-approved or the unit qualifies for Single-Unit Approval, and you'll live in it as your primary residence. We check first.
How much down payment do I need in Boynton Beach?+
As little as 3.5% with a 580+ score; gift funds and seller credits can help.
Do you serve all of Palm Beach County?+
Yes — Boynton Beach, West Palm Beach, Boca Raton, Delray Beach, and surrounding communities.
